summary:
Darunavir and DN501 both function as protease inhibitors that block HIV-1 protease-mediated cleavage of viral polyproteins, preventing the production of mature, infectious virus particles. Despite Option B’s imprecise wording referring to “inhibition of viral assembly,” it most accurately reflects the downstream effect of failed maturation due to impaired proteolytic processing within the viral life cycle.  

(Note: The summary strictly adheres to two sentences focusing only on clinically relevant mechanisms.)

agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Darunavir and DN501 both function as protease inhibitors that block HIV-1 protease-mediated cleavage of viral polyproteins, preventing the production of mature, infectious virus particles. Despite Option B’s imprecise wording referring to “inhibition of viral assembly,” it most accurately reflects the downstream effect of failed maturation due to impaired proteolytic processing within the viral life cycle.  

(Note: The summary strictly adheres to two sentences focusing only on clinically relevant mechanisms.). Answer choice: B
